user_goal: 在 B015 done 之后做 B010 risk parity HRP 升级（BL-B010-S3）：给 RiskParityParameters 加 weighting_method Literal['inverse_volatility', 'hrp']（默认 inverse_volatility，B010 signoff 行为 bit-for-bit 保留），新增 pure-stdlib HRP 算法模块（De Prado 2016：correlation → distance → single-linkage clustering → quasi-diagonalize → recursive bisection；无 scipy/numpy/pandas/sklearn）；B010 backtest workflow dispatch；在 B014 真实 snapshot 上对比 HRP vs inverse-vol vs static 60/40，看 HRP 是否缩窄 B014 cross-strategy 发现的 absolute-return gap。**不修改 B011/B012/B013/B014/B015 strategy code**；B011 master portfolio risk_parity sleeve 默认走 inverse-vol 自动保持 invariants。保留 fixture/mock-first 默认 CI、no-live/no-secret-in-strategy/no-broker/no-paper/no-AI guards。

session_notes.planner: 2026-05-14 B016 planning: B015 done wrap-up confirmed (6/6 PASS); user selected BL-B010-S3 HRP upgrade as next batch direction. Rationale: B010 plain inverse-vol is reused as B011 master portfolio risk_parity sleeve and B013 L2 engine; upgrading once benefits multiple downstream paths. arxiv 2026 research material (Beyond De Prado and Cotton, Hierarchical Min Variance, Topological RP) strongly supports HRP variants over plain inverse-vol on out-of-sample Sharpe + drawdown + stability. Plus the B014 cross-strategy finding (B010 underperformed static 60/40 by ~25pp in 2020-06..2022-12) motivates testing whether correlation-aware HRP closes some of that gap on the same real snapshot. Project constraint check: trade/ modules are pure-stdlib zero-third-party-deps (only yfinance, in scripts/ only). HRP must be implemented in pure stdlib (math + statistics + dataclasses + typing) — no scipy/numpy/pandas/sklearn. Small algorithm, ~200 lines, reviewable. Decisions captured: (1) weighting_method tightened from free str to Literal['inverse_volatility', 'hrp'] with default 'inverse_volatility'; (2) HRP lives in new trade/strategies/risk_parity_hrp.py; (3) B010 workflow dispatches based on policy; (4) comparative backtest harness reuses B014 snapshot + sidecar baseline; (5) default must reproduce B010 signoff bit-for-bit (backwards-compat hard requirement); (6) B011/B012/B013/B014/B015 strategy code untouched; (7) no scipy/numpy/pandas/sklearn imports anywhere. Wrote B016 spec and features.json (6 features: F001-F005 generator + F006 codex; mixed batch). Status moved to building for Generator F001.
session_notes.generator: 2026-05-14 B016 F001+F002 done. F001: weighting_method Literal + __post_init__ validation in trade/strategies/risk_parity.py; removed redundant validate_risk_parity_parameters check that would have blocked 'hrp'. F002: trade/strategies/risk_parity_hrp.py implements De Prado (2016) HRP in pure stdlib — math/statistics/dataclasses/typing/collections.abc only, NO scipy/numpy/pandas/sklearn/networkx. Pipeline functions: compute_correlation_matrix, compute_distance_matrix (sqrt(0.5*(1-corr))), single_linkage_clustering (returns ClusterNode tree; deterministic tie-break via iteration order on `active` list, smaller original ids first), quasi_diagonalize (iterative pre-order using stack, walks left subtree first), recursive_bisection (alpha = 1 - V_L/(V_L+V_R), V is inverse-variance-weighted portfolio variance over cluster), compute_hrp_weights top-level entry. ClusterNode dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True) carries members tuple for debug + assertions. Edge cases: n=1 trivial, n=2 inverse-variance fast-path skipping clustering, n>=3 full pipeline, zero variance → ValueError, perfect-correlation pair handled via d=0. Final normalization guards floating-point drift. Tests in tests/unit/test_risk_parity_hrp.py (39 tests): correlation diagonal/symmetry/identical/anticorrelated/linear/zero-var-rejection/short/ragged; distance perfect-pos/perfect-neg/zero/diagonal/symmetry/De-Prado-formula; single linkage 1/2/3/4-leaf canned + tied determinism + min-linkage trace + empty-rejection; quasi-diagonalize 1-leaf + 3-leaf pre-order + balanced; recursive_bisection equal-var/inverse-var/four-equal/De-Prado-alpha-formula/length-mismatch; end-to-end n∈{1,2,3,5,9} + zero-var rejection + duplicate-symbols + count-mismatch + determinism (3 runs) + perfect-corr-pair + low-vol-gets-higher-weight invariant. Initial test wrote naive expectations [0,1,2,3] for some clustering scenarios; corrected after tracing the actual deterministic merge order (e.g., 3-leaf produces pre-order [2,0,1] because final merge has leaf 2 as left, {0,1} as right; 4-leaf min-linkage produces [3,0,1,2]). Both are valid quasi-diagonal orderings — correlated cluster {1,2} still adjacent in the final ordering. Full sweep: 483 tests PASS, mypy strict clean (files=['trade']), ruff clean, compileall clean. Next: F003 B010 workflow dispatcher.
